Bead agent-20068588 (FZ-037 Audit external package managers) — no code changes needed.

Status: AC already satisfied by an existing committed audit document.

Evidence:
- File `.ddx/notes/FZ-037-external-package-manager-audit.md` is present in the
  working tree at base rev bbfeb1c and was originally introduced by commit
  6e62e75 ("docs: audit external package manager and release surfaces
  [agent-20068588]"). The commit is reachable from HEAD
  (`git log -- .ddx/notes/FZ-037-external-package-manager-audit.md`).

AC walk-through ("Audit doc lists each ecosystem with status present/absent/N/A
and follow-up bead IDs for any present external surface"):
- Each ecosystem named in the bead description (Homebrew, asdf, scoop, snap,
  AUR, codesign, notarization) appears in the audit table with explicit
  status. Additional surfaces (Flatpak, Chocolatey, Debian/RPM, npm/PyPI,
  Docker, Windows Store) are also enumerated for completeness.
- The two present surfaces (GitHub Releases workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`
  and `install.sh`) are documented; both already use Fizeau-correct names
  (binary `fiz`, repo `easel/fizeau`), so no rename follow-up beads
  are required. Functional install.sh enhancements are cross-referenced to
  FZ-033 (`agent-fee0000d`).
- All other listed surfaces are marked `absent` (with negative evidence:
  no `Formula/`, no `PKGBUILD`, no `snapcraft.yaml`, no `Dockerfile`, no
  notarization/codesign step, etc.) or `N/A` (Windows Store — no Windows
  build target).

Spot-checked at the current base rev:
- `install.sh` still has `REPO="easel/fizeau"` and
  `BINARY_NAME="${BINARY_NAME:-fiz}"`.
- `.github/workflows/` contains only `ci.yml`, `release.yml`, `website.yml`.
- No `Formula/`, `PKGBUILD`, `snapcraft.yaml`, `Dockerfile`, `*.nuspec`, or
  similar packaging artifacts exist outside `node_modules`.

Conclusion: the audit deliverable is already merged and remains accurate at
the current base rev. There is nothing new to commit. The bead can be closed
on the strength of the existing artifact.
